children.text: to provide policy direction for and to conduct, supervise, and coordinate audits and investigations relating to the programs and operations of the establishment;

to review existing and proposed legislation and regulations relating to programs and operations of the establishment and to make recommendations in the semiannual reports required by concerning the impact of the legislation and regulations on the economy and efficiency in the administration of programs and operations administered or financed by the establishment, or the prevention and detection of fraud and abuse in the programs and operations;section 405(b) of this title

to recommend policies for, and to conduct, supervise, or coordinate other activities carried out or financed by, the establishment for the purpose of promoting economy and efficiency in the administration of, or preventing and detecting fraud and abuse in, its programs and operations;

children.label: A
children.text: all matters relating to the promotion of economy and efficiency in the administration of, or the prevention and detection of fraud and abuse in, programs and operations administered or financed by the establishment; or

children.label: B
children.text: the identification and prosecution of participants in fraud or abuse referred to in subparagraph (A); and

to keep the head of the establishment and Congress fully and currently informed, by means of the reports required by and otherwise, concerning fraud and other serious problems, abuses, and deficiencies relating to the administration of programs and operations administered or financed by the establishment, to recommend corrective action concerning the problems, abuses, and deficiencies, and to report on the progress made in implementing the corrective action.section 405 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: comply with standards established by the Comptroller General of the United States for audits of Federal establishments, organizations, programs, activities, and functions;

children.label: B
children.text: establish guidelines for determining when it shall be appropriate to use non-Federal auditors; and

children.label: C
children.text: take appropriate steps to ensure that any work performed by non-Federal auditors complies with the standards established by the Comptroller General as described in paragraph (1).1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: .—Reviews performed exclusively by audit entities in federal government
children.label: 2 .—Reviews performed exclusively by audit entities in federal government
children.text: For purposes of determining compliance with paragraph (1)(A) with respect to whether internal quality controls are in place and operating and whether established audit standards, policies, and procedures are being followed by Offices of Inspector General of establishments defined under , Offices of Inspector General of designated Federal entities defined under , and any audit office established within a Federal entity defined under , reviews shall be performed exclusively by an audit entity in the Federal Government, including the Government Accountability Office or the Office of Inspector General of each establishment defined under , or the Office of Inspector General of each designated Federal entity defined under .section 401 of this titlesection 415(a) of this titlesection 415(a) of this titlesection 401 of this titlesection 415(a) of this title

children.heading: .—Effective Coordination and Cooperation
children.label: c .—Effective Coordination and Cooperation
children.text: In carrying out the duties and responsibilities established under this chapter, each Inspector General shall give particular regard to the activities of the Comptroller General of the United States with a view toward avoiding duplication and ensuring effective coordination and cooperation.

children.heading: .—Reporting Violation of Federal Criminal Law
children.label: d .—Reporting Violation of Federal Criminal Law
children.text: In carrying out the duties and responsibilities established under this chapter, each Inspector General shall report expeditiously to the Attorney General whenever the Inspector General has reasonable grounds to believe there has been a violation of Federal criminal law.

children.label: ii
children.text: the congressional committees of jurisdiction; and

children.label: iii
children.text: if the recommendation for corrective action was initiated upon request by an individual or entity other than the Inspector General, that individual or entity;

children.label: B
children.text: may submit the document making a recommendation for corrective action to any Member of Congress upon request; and

children.label: C
children.text: not later than 3 days after the recommendation for corrective action is submitted in final form to the head of the establishment, post the document making a recommendation for corrective action on the website of the Office of Inspector General.

children.heading: .—Public disclosure otherwise prohibited by law
children.label: 2 .—Public disclosure otherwise prohibited by law
children.text: Nothing in this subsection shall be construed as authorizing an Inspector General to publicly disclose information otherwise prohibited from disclosure by law.
